debug_info.goal:
α : Type
hα : OracleComp.SampleableType α
x : Bool
⊢ x ∈
    support
      (@OracleComp.HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect! (Vector Bool 2) Bool (OracleComp.hasUniformSelectVector Bool 1)
        #v[true, false])

failed_attempts: aesop

simp [support, DiscreteTopology.tsum_eq_single x]
    <;>
    (try cases x <;> simp [hasUniformSelectVector, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one])
    <;>
    (try norm_num)
    <;>
    (try aesop)

fin_cases x <;>
      simp [Vector.get, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
    <;> norm_num
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by sorry
  probFailure_selectElem := by sorry

have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp_all
    cases h₁ with
    | inl h₁ =>
      rw [h₁]
      simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> rfl
    | inr h₁ =>
      rw [h₁]
      simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> rfl
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    -- Prove that the probability of selecting x is the same as y
    have h₁ : {x : Bool // x ∈ ({true, false} : Set Bool)} = Set.univ := by
      ext x
      simp [Finset.mem_coe]
      <;> cases x <;> simp_all
    simp_all [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
    <;> norm_num <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      rcases x with (_ | _) <;> rcases y with (_ | _) <;> simp_all [probOutput]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> rfl
    })
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    -- Prove that the probability of failure is 0
    simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> rfl

have h : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      have : x = true ∨ x = false := by
        cases x <;> simp_all
      tauto
    cases h with
    | inl h =>
      rw [h]
      simp [support, $(!·), ProbComp, OptionT]
      <;> decide
    | inr h =>
      rw [h]
      simp [support, $(!·), ProbComp, OptionT]
      <;> decide
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    have h : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      have : x = true ∨ x = false := by
        cases x <;> simp_all
      tauto
    have h' : y = true ∨ y = false := by
      have : y = true ∨ y = false := by
        cases y <;> simp_all
      tauto
    rcases h with (rfl | rfl) <;> rcases h' with (rfl | rfl) <;> simp [$(·), ProbComp, OptionT] <;>
      decide
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    simp [$(·), ProbComp, OptionT]
    <;> decide

have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
    cases h₁ with
    | inl hx =>
      simp_all [hx, SUPPORT, evalDist, uniformSelectVector_def, evalDist_uniformSelectVector, Function.funext_iff]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> aesop
    | inr hx =>
      simp_all [hx, SUPPORT, evalDist, uniformSelectVector_def, evalDist_uniformSelectVector, Function.funext_iff]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> aesop
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
    have h₂ : y = true ∨ y = false := by
      cases y <;> simp
    simp [SUPPORT, evalDist, uniformSelectVector_def, evalDist_uniformSelectVector, Function.funext_iff] at *
    <;>
    (try {
      cases h₁ <;> cases h₂ <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true}) <;> norm_num <;> aesop
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [SUPPORT, evalDist, uniformSelectVector_def, evalDist_uniformSelectVector, Function.funext_iff]
      <;> norm_num <;> aesop
    })
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    have h₁ : (0 : ℕ) < 2 := by norm_num
    simp [SUPPORT, evalDist, uniformSelectVector_def, evalDist_uniformSelectVector, Function.funext_iff]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> aesop

have h : x ∈ support (@OracleComp.HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect! (Vector Bool 2) Bool (OracleComp.hasUniformSelectVector Bool 1) #v[true, false]) := by
      match x with
      | true =>
        -- Case x = true
        have h₁ : (true : Bool) ∈ support (@OracleComp.HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect! (Vector Bool 2) Bool (OracleComp.hasUniformSelectVector Bool 1) #v[true, false]) := by
          -- Show that the element true is in the support by considering index 0
          -- (since the vector is [true, false], index 0 is true)
          classical
          simp [support, pmf_apply, Vector.get, Fin.forall_fin_succ, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
          <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try {
            use ⟨0, by decide⟩
            <;> simp [Vector.get]
            <;> norm_num
          })
        exact h₁
      | false =>
        -- Case x = false
        have h₁ : (false : Bool) ∈ support (@OracleComp.HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect! (Vector Bool 2) Bool (OracleComp.hasUniformSelectVector Bool 1) #v[true, false]) := by
          -- Show that the element false is in the support by considering index 1
          -- (since the vector is [true, false], index 1 is false)
          classical
          simp [support, pmf_apply, Vector.get, Fin.forall_fin_succ, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
          <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try {
            use ⟨1, by decide⟩
            <;> simp [Vector.get]
            <;> norm_num
          })
        exact h₁
    simpa [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!] using h
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    simp [SelectElem.eq]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
    })
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    simp [SelectElem.ne]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all (config := {decide := true})
    })

cases x <;> decide
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by sorry
  probFailure_selectElem := by sorry

0, this is true.
    sorry
  · -- Case x = false
    -- Similarly, we need to show that there exists an i : Fin 2 such that (Vector.ofFn ![true, false]).get i = false.
    -- For i = 1, this is true.
    sorry

have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
    rcases h₁ with (rfl | rfl)
    · -- Case x = true
      simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
      <;> rfl
    · -- Case x = false
      simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
      <;> rfl
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
    have h₂ : y = true ∨ y = false := by
      cases y <;> simp
    rcases h₁ with (rfl | rfl) <;> rcases h₂ with (rfl | rfl) <;> simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      rfl
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [selectEq, smapAnd, smapOr]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      rfl
    })
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get]
    <;>
    rfl

simp [support, HasUniformSelect!]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      fin_cases x <;> decide
    }) <;>
    (try {
      fin_cases x <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
    })
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    fin_cases x <;> fin_cases y <;>
      simp [probOutput, pmf_eq, uniformSelect!] <;>
      norm_num <;>
      rfl
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    simp [probFailure, pmf_eq, uniformSelect!]
    <;>
    norm_num

have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x
      · exact Or.inr rfl
      · exact Or.inl rfl
    -- We need to show that x is in the support of the uniformSelect! instance for the vector #v[true, false]
    have h₂ : x ∈ (Vector.toList #v[true, false]).toFinset := by
      simp_all [Vector.toList_cons, Vector.toList_nil, Finset.mem_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
      <;> aesop
    -- Since x is in the toFinset of the vector, it must be in the support of the uniformSelect! instance
    simp_all [support, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Vector.toList_cons, Vector.toList_nil, Finset.mem_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [uniformSelectVector_def, uniformSelectList_def, ProbM.nat_cast, Finset.mem_coe, List.mem_toFinset]) <;>
    (try aesop)
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    simp [sample, PCpu.hack]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try
      {
        cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all [sample, PCpu.hack]
        <;>
        (try decide)
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        aesop
      })
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    simp [sample, PCpu.hack]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [sample, PCpu.hack]
        <;>
        (try decide)
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        aesop
      })

have h : x = true ∨ x = false := by cases x <;> simp
    rcases h with (rfl | rfl)
    · -- Case x = true
      have h₁ : (true : Bool) ∈ support ($! #v[true, false]) := by
        -- Prove that true is in the support of $! #v[true, false]
        have h₂ : (0 : Fin 2) = (0 : Fin 2) := rfl
        have h₃ : (true : Bool) = ((#v[true, false] : Vector Bool 2)[(0 : Fin 2)] : Bool) := by simp
        simp_all [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
        <;> decide
      exact h₁
    · -- Case x = false
      have h₁ : (false : Bool) ∈ support ($! #v[true, false]) := by
        -- Prove that false is in the support of $! #v[true, false]
        have h₂ : (1 : Fin 2) = (1 : Fin 2) := rfl
        have h₃ : (false : Bool) = ((#v[true, false] : Vector Bool 2)[(1 : Fin 2)] : Bool) := by simp
        simp_all [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Vector.get, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
        <;> decide
      exact h₁
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    -- Prove that the probability of outputting x is the same as y
    have h₁ : Pr[= x | $! #v[true, false]] = (1 : ℝ≥0∞) / 2 := by
      simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, probOutput_map_eq_sum_fintype_ite, Fin.sum_univ_succ]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try {
        fin_cases x <;> simp [probOutput_map_eq_sum_fintype_ite, Fin.sum_univ_succ] <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try {
            norm_num
            <;>
            rfl
          })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        norm_num
        <;>
        rfl
      })
    have h₂ : Pr[= y | $! #v[true, false]] = (1 : ℝ≥0∞) / 2 := by
      simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, probOutput_map_eq_sum_fintype_ite, Fin.sum_univ_succ]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try {
        fin_cases y <;> simp [probOutput_map_eq_sum_fintype_ite, Fin.sum_univ_succ] <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try {
            norm_num
            <;>
            rfl
          })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        norm_num
        <;>
        rfl
      })
    -- Since both probabilities are 1/2, they are equal
    linarith
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    -- Prove that the probability of failure is 0
    simp [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, probFailure_map_eq]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      simp [probFailure_map_eq]
      <;>
      (try decide)
    })

2. Case `x = false`: show that `false` is in the support.

Alternatively, perhaps we need to use some properties of the `support` of the distribution. 

But perhaps we don't need to. 

Given that we don't have access to Lean's definitions, perhaps we should proceed as follows:

1. For any `x : Bool`, we know that either `x = true` or `x = false`.
2. The vector is `#v[true, false]`, so it contains both values.
3. The uniform selection from the vector is done by selecting one of the two indices, so it picks either `true` or `false`, hence both are in the support. 

But formally, perhaps we need to prove that if we pick `true` or `false`, they are in the support.

have h : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
      <;> (try decide)
      <;> (try { left; rfl })
      <;> (try { right; rfl })
    
    have h₁ : x ∈ support ($! #v[true, false]) := by
      have h₂ : x = true ∨ x = false := h
      cases h₂ with
      | inl h₂ =>
        -- Case: x = true
        have h₃ : (true : Bool) ∈ support ($! #v[true, false]) := by
          -- Show that true is in the support
          simp [HasUniformSelect!, OptionT.support, support, Function.comp_apply, Pi.zero_apply,
            Pi.one_apply, Vector.get]
          <;>
            (try decide) <;>
            (try
              {
                simp_all [Fintype.card_fin, ENNReal.coe_ne_zero, Nat.cast_eq_zero]
                <;>
                  decide
              })
          <;>
            (try
              {
                simp_all [Fintype.card_fin, ENNReal.coe_ne_zero, Nat.cast_eq_zero]
                <;>
                  norm_num
                <;>
                  aesop
              })
        -- Since x = true, we can use the above result
        rw [h₂] at *
        exact h₃
      | inr h₂ =>
        -- Case: x = false
        have h₃ : (false : Bool) ∈ support ($! #v[true, false]) := by
          -- Show that false is in the support
          simp [HasUniformSelect!, OptionT.support, support, Function.comp_apply, Pi.zero_apply,
            Pi.one_apply, Vector.get]
          <;>
            (try decide) <;>
            (try
              {
                simp_all [Fintype.card_fin, ENNReal.coe_ne_zero, Nat.cast_eq_zero]
                <;>
                  decide
              })
          <;>
            (try
              {
                simp_all [Fintype.card_fin, ENNReal.coe_ne_zero, Nat.cast_eq_zero]
                <;>
                  norm_num
                <;>
                  aesop
              })
        -- Since x = false, we can use the above result
        rw [h₂] at *
        exact h₃
    
    exact h₁

      cases x <;> simp
      <;> aesop
    
    have h₁ : x ∈ support (@HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect! (Vector Bool 2) Bool (hasUniformSelectVector Bool 1) #v[true, false]) := by
      simp only [HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, hasUniformSelectVector] at *
      cases h with

        -- Case: x = true
        simp_all [Vector.get, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]

        (try decide) <;>

          (try decide)
